decisions.description: Add synchronous validation hooks to the checkout pipeline that can return errors and abort mutations, replacing the current notification-only PluginManager hooks.
Plugin validate() methods return a list of error objects with field, message, and optional metadata; the mutation maps them to GraphQL errors.
Extend the CheckoutError GraphQL type with an EXTERNAL_VALIDATION_ERROR code and a metadata field for plugin-specific details.
Run the new pre-validation hook after input cleaning but before the database save in checkout mutations like checkoutCreate.
Pass plugins serialized checkout data rather than raw querysets, maintaining the same security boundary used for webhook payloads.
Reuse the existing sync-webhook pattern from tax calculations: the checkout mutation calls PluginManager, which issues the HTTP request to the external validation service and waits for the response.
Cache validation results in Redis keyed by the Checkout's last_change timestamp with a TTL, and invalidate whenever lines, addresses, or shipping method change.
Implement a per-app per-event-type circuit breaker using a Redis sliding window that skips a validation after three consecutive timeouts so it cannot block checkouts indefinitely.
Route app-registered validation webhooks through the existing WebhookPlugin bridge that already handles tax-calculation app webhooks.
Audit-log validation rejections so merchants can debug blocked checkouts after the fact.

action_items.text: Write the security spec for plugin data-access boundaries and circuit-breaker behavior
Prototype the validation hooks and test against the existing tax service integration
Deliver base implementation for review by end of sprint; check-in Thursday on the plugin changes (critical path)
